properties.remark: REPORT FROM MPING: 1-INCH TREE LIMBS BROKEN; SHINGLES BLOWN OFF.
MUDSLIDES IN SOUTHBOUND LANES NEAR 2500 S LYTLE CREEK RD. HEAVY RAIN IN THE AREA.
ORANGE COUNTY PUBLIC WORKS CLEARED MUD AND ROCKS ONE FOOT DEEP FROM SILVERADO CANYON ROAD NEAR OLIVE DRIVE IN THE BOND FIRE BURN SCAR.
SEVERAL ROCK SLIDES ON MT BALDY ROAD NEAR HILL AVENUE, BUT NOT ENOUGH TO BLOCK THE ROAD AND PREVENT VEHICLES FROM PASSING.
GENE AUTRY TRAIL WAS CLOSED JUST SOUTH OF INTERSTATE 10 DUE TO BLOWING DUST.
NORTH GENE AUTRY TRAIL CLOSED THROUGH THE WASH DUE TO WIND AND SAND, BETWEEN SALIVA AND E VIA ESCUELA.
NORTH INDIAN CANYON ROAD CLOSED BETWEEN GARNET AVE AND SUNRISE PKWY DUE TO SAND BUILD UP AND POOR VISIBILITY.

BLOWING SAND IS IMPACTING INTERSATE 10 WITH WORSE CONDITIONS OCCURRING BETWEEN BOB HOPE DRIVE IN THOUSAND PALMS TO INDIO. VISIBILITY IS ESTIMATED TO BE AS LOW AS A 1/2 MILE BASED OFF CALTRANS WEBCAMS.
CALTRANS INTERSTATE 10 WEBCAM SHOWING EXTREME VISIBILITY RESTRICTIONS IN BLOWING SAND. VISIBILITY ESTIMATED TO BE LESS THAN 1/8 MILE.
ROADWAY FLOODING, MOSTLY IN THE #1 LANE, ON THE I-10 E NEAR MILE MARKER 18.
ROCKS ON ROADWAY AT JENKS LAKE ROAD AND HIGHWAY 38. HEAVY RAIN HAS FALLEN IN THIS AREA WITH AT LEAST 0.50 TOTALS, HIGHER TO THE EAST IN THE SANTA ANA RIVER WATERSHED AND BARTON FLATS.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS DEBRIS FLOW REPORT FROM 5 W BIG BEAR LAKE. ROCK SLIDE COVERING BOTH LANES OF HIGHWAY 18. MULTIPLE VEHICLES HIT BY ROCKS AND DAMAGED. NEAR 1 INCH OF RAIN RECORDED IN GAGES IN 60 MINUTES.
ROCK SLIDE COVERING BOTH LANES OF HIGHWAY 18. MULTIPLE VEHICLES HIT BY ROCKS AND DAMAGED.
ROCKS SLIDING INTO LANES. ONE VEHICLE IMPACTED.

CORRECTS AND UPDATED PREVIOUS FLOOD REPORT FROM 1 N LUCERNE VALLEY. SR 247 CLOSED AT RABBIT SPRINGS ROAD NORTHBOUND DUE TO FLOODING. VERY HEAVY RAIN FELL IN THIS AREA CAUSING FLASH FLOODING AND DEBRIS FLOWS ON STATE ROUTE 247 WHICH WAS CLOSED.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS FLOOD REPORT FROM 4 SW FOREST FALLS. A CAR WAS REPORTED STUCK IN FLOOD WATERS AT OAK GLEN ROAD AND POTATO CANYON ROAD. 10 HOMES WERE SHELTER IN PLACE DUE TO FLASH FLOOD OF POTATO CANYON FROM BIRCH CREEK. YUCAIPA RIDGE RECORDED 1.5 INCHES IN AROUND 60 MINUTES, TOTAL OF 1.8 INCHES FROM 2 THUNDERSTORMS. STORM SURVEY COMPLETED AT 11 AM AUGUST 1.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS FLOOD REPORT FROM 4 SW FOREST FALLS. POTATO CANYON ROAD AT BIRCH CREEK COMPLETELY COVERED IN MUD AND DEBRIS. SAN BERNARDINO COUNTY FIRE POSTED VIDEO OF FLOODING ON SCENE. ROAD CLOSED SATURDAY NIGHT INTO SUNDAY MORNING AT OAK GLEN ROAD. 10 RESIDENTS WERE SHELTER IN PLACE. 1.8 INCHES OF RAIN OCCURRED AT YUCAIPA RIDGE UPSTREAM, ABOUT 1.2 INCH IN 60 MINUTES BETWEEN 530 AND 630 PM. EL DORADO FIRE SCAR AFFECTED IN UPSTREAM RAINFALL.

CORRECTS TIME OF PREVIOUS FLASH FLOOD REPORT FROM 5 N FOREST FALLS. WATER ACTIVELY FLOWING ACROSS HIGHWAY 38. CALTRANS IN PROCESS OF CLEARING DEBRIS AND ROCKS. ROADWAY IS CLOSED. VEHICLES WERE STUCK AND RESCUE PRIOR TO 1 PM. HIGHWAY 38 AT HILL RANCH ROAD.
RESCUE EFFORT ONGOING WEST OF JENKS LAKE ON HWY 38 WITH MULTIPLE CARS STUCK. HWY 38 IS CLOSED BETWEEN VALLEY OF THE FALLS TO LAKE WILLIAMS.
WATER ACTIVELY FLOWING ACROSS HIGHWAY 38. CALTRANS IN PROCESS OF CLEARING DEBRIS AND ROCKS. ROADWAY IS CLOSED. VEHICLES WERE STUCK AND RESCUE PRIOR TO 1 PM. HIGHWAY 38 AT HILL RANCH ROAD.
CAR STALLED IN 4 FT OF WATER AT HIGHWAY 38 AT STANFIELD CUTOFF. PARTIES TRAPPED INSIDE WERE RESCUED BY FIRE DEPT. ROAD WAS CLOSED TO CLEAR DEBRIS.

UPDATED PREVIOUS FLASH FLOOD REPORT FROM 2 W BIG BEAR CITY IN FAWNSKIN. CAR STALLED IN 4 FT OF WATER AT HIGHWAY 38 AT STANFIELD CUTOFF. PARTIES TRAPPED INSIDE WERE RESCUED BY FIRE DEPT. ROAD WAS CLOSED TO CLEAR DEBRIS. SEVERAL CARS REPORTED STUCK IN MUD BY CALTRANS. RADAR ESTIMATE 2 TO 4 INCHES ALONG HIGHWAY 38, NORTH SIDE BIG BEAR LAKE, TO FAWNSKIN. 3 GAGES RECORDED 2 INCHES TOTAL IN 2 DAYS. 2.38 INCHES AT BIG BEAR RANGER STATION.

UPDATES PREVIOUS FLOOD REPORT FROM 2 E BIG BEAR CITY. ROADWAY FLOODING NEAR 1509 SHAY RD IN BIG BEAR CITY. NORTH AND SOUTH SIDE OF SHAY ROAD WAS FLOODED. STORM SURVEY BY WCM AT 2 PM SHOWED SEVERAL INCHES OF WATER STILL FLOWING OVER SHAY ROAD. RADAR ESTIMATED 2 TO 3 INCHES IN LESS THAN 3 HOURS. WATER FLOWED AROUND NUMEROUS HOMES, FLOWING INTO BALDWIN LAKE. NEARBY BIG BEAR YARD REPORTED 1.80 INCHES.
2-3 OF WATER FLOODING ACROSS THE RDWY AT SR18 AND JOHNSON RD.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS FLASH FLOOD REPORT FROM 5 N PHELAN. 2-3 FEET OF WATER FLOODING ACROSS THE RDWY AT SR18 AND JOHNSON RD.
CHP REPORT OF ROCKS IN THE EASTBOUND LANDS OF HIGHWAY 74 NEAR PINYON PINES. HEAVY RAIN ONGOING IN THE AREA AT THE TIME.
CHP REPORT OF MUD AND WATER ACROSS BOTH LANES OF HIGHWAY 18. RADAR ESTIMATES OF ONE HOUR RAINFALL BETWEEN ONE-QUARTER TO ONE-HALF INCH IN THIS AREA.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND GST REPORT FROM 4 SSE PINE VALLEY. HORSE SDHGPA MESONET STATION.

MORE THAN 200 PEOPLE WERE STRANDED AT THE PALM SPRINGS AERIAL TRAMWAY ON MONDAY EVENING AS HEAVY RAIN IN THE SAN JACINTO MOUNTAINS AND STATE PARK CAUSED IMPASSIBLE MUDFLOWS ALONG THE EXIT ROAD FROM THE VALLEY STATION. THE TRAM IS CLOSED TUESDAY FOR INSPECTION OF DAMAGE TO THE ROADWAY AND HIKING AREAS. OPERATIONS OF PALM SPRINGS TRAM SAID MOUNTAIN DEVELOPED INTO MUD IN THE CREEK AREA AT THE VALLEY STATION, THEN BEGAN FLOWING DOWN EAST OF THE STATION ONTO THE ROAD. THE FLOW APPEARED TO BE MOSTLY MUD, AND NOT DEBRIS, WHICH WAS THE CASE IN 2019 WHEN RAINS CAUSED A SIMILAR CLOSURE. 1.99 INCHES RAINFALL MEASURED AT MT SAN JACINTO PEAK RAWS.
BROADCAST MEDIA REPORTS FLASH FLOODING AND A MUD FLOW AT LONG VALLEY STATION OF THE PALM SPRINGS TRAM. WATER FLOWING OVER ROAD WITH ROCK AND DEBRIS.
5 FOOT OAK TREE DOWN ON BOTH LANES OLD HIGHWAY 80 AND DEODAR.

MULTIPLE ROCKS SLIDES ON HIGHWAY 22. 4.6 INCHES OF RAIN AT RANCHITA AND 1.5 INCHES AT BORREGO SPRINGS.

TORNADO LANDSPOUTS USUALLY DO NOT HAVE SUPERCELL MESOCYCLONES VISIBLE IN CLOUDS, FUNNEL CLOUD OR ON RADAR. NUMEROUS PHOTOS AND VIDEOS CAPTURED THE LANDSPOUT EXTENDING UP INTO THE BASE OF THE THUNDERSTORM FLAT CLOUD BASE (NUMEROUS LIGHTNING STRIKES DETECTED) WHICH MOVED EAST TO WEST FROM MENIFEE TO SOUTHERN CANYON LAKE TO LAKE ELISINORE ALONG HIGHWAY 78. HAIL UP TO HALF INCH ALSO REPORTED ALONG WITH VERY HEAVY RAIN SHOWN. DAMAGE APPEARED TO BE MINOR BUT INFORMATION IS BEING COLLECTED. THE EF0 LANDSPOUT TRACKED ACROSS MOSTLY DIRT OPEN SPACE BEFORE DISSIPATING NEAR I-15. THIS AREA IS KNOWN AS ELSINORE CONVERGENCE ZONE AND HAS HAD SEVERAL LANDSPOUTS IN PAST 20 YEARS REPORTED.
DOWNED TREES IN MENIFEE, TWO WHICH APPEARED DEAD, TWO OF WHICH APPEARED TO BE HALF DEAD. WIND GUSTS MEASURED BETWEEN 40 AND 46 MPH IN MENIFEE AT THE TIME.
